chapter_goal: Use connectivity and incidence geometry to explain why families of cycles can force strong equivalence results.
checks: incidence graph is connected under the model hypotheses
equivalence classes merge exactly along connected paths
lab: Remove incidence edges and identify which algebraic-equivalence conclusions no longer follow.
library_routes: NetworkX for incidence and connectivity graphs
pitfalls: assuming connectivity without the numerical hypotheses
forgetting the role of parameter-space dimension
proof_moves: translate geometric connectivity into algebraic control
use incidence correspondences to propagate cycle relations
compare equivalence relations through normal functions
visual_focus: An incidence graph connects cycles, hypersurfaces, and parameter spaces to show how equivalence propagates.
